design_philosophy.core_loop: Execute hockey plays while navigating environmental hazards, managing special abilities, and adapting to dynamic field conditions that kill players
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between aggressive play and survival against field hazards, opposing team violence, and environmental chaos
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage a chaotic, anarchic mutant hockey team in a darkly comedic violent sport where traditional rules are subverted
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately inverts sports game conventions: hockey is not a skill simulation but a death game where hazards, landmines, holes, and corpses are core mechanics. Death is celebrated rather than penalized. Embraces gore, mutation, and rule-breaking (bribable refs, demon goalies) as primary gameplay vectors rather than simulation authenticity.

difficulty.lives_system: Players can die on ice; corpses remain and become hazards for other players. Death is frequent and expected
difficulty.checkpointing: Game structured by periods with giant slug 'ice resurfacer' clearing debris between periods; implies checkpoint/reset between phases
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Teams ranked 0-6 skulls suggesting selectable difficulty tiers; player choice of team affects challenge level. Hazard density and goalie selection (demon vs standard) provide tactical difficulty modulation

progression.upgrade_system: Team/roster selection with ranked difficulty skulls (0-6). Goalie substitution (standard to demon head) acts as tactical progression. Crowd drops powerups that can be picked up and used
progression.unlock_mechanism: Different mutant species teams (robots, undead skeletons, trolls) appear to be selectable from start; no evidence of unlocking
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer vs single-player modes enable competitive replay
Six skull difficulty tiers support escalating challenge attempts
Random crowd weapon/powerup drops encourage varied playthroughs
Fight minigames provide secondary skill-testing loop

world_building_techniques: Mutant team composition (robots, skeletons, trolls) establishes dark fantasy sports universe
Hazard placement (landmines, holes, corpses) reinforces 'death game' atmosphere
Demon goalie visual metaphor for rule inversion and chaos
Crowd hostility (throwing weapons) implies hostile spectator culture
Giant slug ice resurfacer is absurdist detail reinforcing tone

faction_indicators: Three selectable mutant species (robots, undead, trolls) suggest faction-based team identity
Team skull rankings (0-6) create visible power hierarchy
danger_signals: Death mechanics and corpse hazards normalize violence as game mechanic
Fight minigames with knockout penalties encourage aggressive play
Landmines and environmental kill zones indicate lethal arena design
environmental_storytelling: Littered hazards on ice establish chaotic, poorly maintained arena
Corpse persistence on ice floor conveys high death rate normalization
Demon goalie visual replaces traditional hockey net with grotesque alternative
Crowd weapon drops imply spectator participation in player harm
